title: The Crest-Jewel of Wisdom
subtitle: Vivekachudamani
author: Shankaracharya (Adi Shankara)
excerpt: A luminous poetic manual of Advaita Vedanta, attributed to Shankara, guiding the seeker through discrimination (viveka) between the real and the unreal to the direct recognition of the identity of Atman and Brahman.
keyIdeas: Brahman is real, the world is superimposed appearance
Discrimination (viveka) is the crown jewel of spiritual practice
The fourfold qualification (sadhana chatushtaya) is prerequisite for inquiry
The five sheaths (koshas) conceal the Self through layers of misidentification
Liberation (moksha) is attained through knowledge alone, not ritual or action
The realized soul (jivanmukta) lives free while still embodied
The mahavakya 'Tat tvam asi' encapsulates the entire teaching
The guru is indispensable for transmission of self-knowledge
keyTakeaways: A human birth, longing for liberation, and a realised teacher are the rarest gifts
Freedom cannot be won by rites, wealth, or austerity — only by knowing the Self
The body, mind, and intellect are not the Self; awareness itself is the Self
Nirvikalpa samadhi is a means, not the end; the end is abiding as Brahman
The jivanmukta acts without doer-ship and rests in unbroken bliss
